name: IMAP / SMTP Email
description: Read, send, and manage email from any standard mailbox.
signup_label: Need a domain or email? Sign up at Hostinger
prompt:
Create a skill for IMAP / SMTP Email.

Use the himalaya CLI to read, send, and manage emails.

## Install himalaya (if not already installed)

curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pimalaya/himalaya/master/install.sh | PREFIX=~/.local sh
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"  # add to ~/.bashrc if not present
himalaya --version

## Write the config file

Create ~/.config/himalaya/config.toml with exactly this content:

[accounts.default]
default = true
email = "{email}"
display-name = "{display_name}"

folder.aliases.inbox  = "INBOX"
folder.aliases.sent   = "Sent"
folder.aliases.drafts = "Drafts"
folder.aliases.trash  = "Trash"

backend.type                = "imap"
backend.host                = "{imap_host}"
backend.port                = 993
backend.encryption.type     = "tls"
backend.login               = "{email}"
backend.auth.type           = "password"
backend.auth.raw            = "{password}"

message.send.backend.type              = "smtp"
message.send.backend.host              = "{smtp_host}"
message.send.backend.port             = 587
message.send.backend.encryption.type   = "start-tls"
message.send.backend.login             = "{email}"
message.send.backend.auth.type         = "password"
message.send.backend.auth.raw          = "{password}"

## Key commands

himalaya folder list
himalaya envelope list
himalaya envelope list -f Sent
himalaya message read <id>
himalaya message reply <id>
himalaya message forward <id>
himalaya message delete <id>
himalaya --output json envelope list

## Sending email

printf 'From: {display_name} <{email}>\nTo: recipient@example.com\nSubject: Hello\n\nBody text here.' | himalaya message send

## Solved cases log

Every time you successfully solve an email task, append a short entry to ~/email_solutions.md:

  ## <short title>  (<date>)
  Goal: <one sentence>
  Key commands: <the himalaya commands or shell pipeline that worked>
  Gotchas: <anything non-obvious>

Read ~/email_solutions.md at the start of each task — if a matching solved case exists, reuse it directly.
